Be anxious about this.<|22.20|> <|22.20|> Be outraged over that.<|26.10|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|1.00|> One of the invitations we find in the sacrament of baptism is to join Christ just under the surface of the water.<|10.00|> <|11.00|> "You can only hear you under there, your own heart beating."<|18.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And all the noise from the shore, the opinions, the dogmas, the criticisms, and empty chatter<|7.00|> <|7.00|> all get drowned out," says the priest and writer Mark Oakley.<|12.88|> <|12.88|> He then goes on to say, "It's just Jesus for a second or two, and then pulled out of<|18.32|> <|18.32|> the water, he takes a deep breath of fresh air.<|21.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> We are told that he then hears a voice, not from the shore, but from heaven.<|6.82|> <|7.84|> This is the one voice that matters and it tells him, you are mine, I love you, you make me happy.<|16.02|> <|16.02|> End quote.<|17.70|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Union. Just under the surface, when he can pay attention, Jesus hears the voice that is always there and that matters most. The voice of union.<|16.00|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> When you and I can sink deep enough beneath the tumult of the winds and waves<|6.92|> <|6.92|> of personal, social, national, and global fragmentation, we are able to know, feel,<|14.64|> <|14.64|> and appreciate the union we are already in with God, one another, and with the earth.<|23.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Sin, if I were writing a theology paper, then can be defined as anything that obscures this union.<|9.60|> <|9.60|> So we come again today as we do very often to the banks of the River Jordan.<|17.70|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Imaginations aflame with the fire of the Holy Spirit embodied in this octagonal baptismal font,<|9.50|> <|9.50|> soon to be consecrated to the glory of God and in memory of Nancy Hawthorne and her beloved grandson Robbie.<|18.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> It is common for baptismal fonts to have eight sides, for in baptism we enter the eighth day of the old creation and the first day of the new.<|17.30|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And as we pray fervently for those who will enter this eighth day with us in just a moment,<|6.36|> <|7.54|> our hearts turn toward the dreams God has for the six people who will be baptized<|13.24|> <|13.24|> over the course of this holy day.<|15.92|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Dreams of holiness and wholeness.<|3.98|> <|5.14|> Dreams of joy and justice.<|7.80|> <|9.22|> Dreams of unconditional love.<|11.68|> <|13.44|> There is nothing better than this.<|15.42|> <|16.76|> Absolutely nothing.<|18.60|> <|20.00|> Amen.<|20.38|>
